steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for one difference between the words “cocoa” and “alpaca” according to the text. The correct answer must be directly supported by what the passage states — not inferred, extended, or introduced from outside the text.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• ""Cocoa" entered English recently, but "alpaca" entered English many centuries ago.": the passage says nothing about when either word entered English. ✗
  • ""Cocoa" can be traced to the Nahuatl language, while "alpaca" can be traced to the Quechua language.": the passage states explicitly that Spanish borrowed “cocoa” from Nahuatl and that “alpaca” comes from Quechua. This is the one difference the text directly identifies. ✓
  • ""Cocoa" changed in meaning significantly when English borrowed the word from Spanish, whereas "alpaca" retained its original meaning.": the passage makes no claim about meaning changes during borrowing. ✗
  • ""Cocoa" is known for being Indigenous in origin, while "alpaca" is mistakenly regarded as Spanish in origin.": the passage says nothing about public misconceptions regarding either word’s origin. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
